hypothesis: Stale-cycle runner hardening becomes safer if runner-stability cards must keep `evidence_commands` unique, so replayable proof cannot silently duplicate recovery or observability steps.
failure_mode: A runner-stability card can currently repeat the same replayable or observability command inside `evidence_commands`, so the proof trail can imply ambiguous retries or padded evidence while still passing boundary and ordering checks.
closed_fail_rule: Keep the slice only if runner-stability cards fail verification unless every `evidence_commands` entry is unique and the replayable proof trail stays bounded.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` accepted runner-stability cards whose `evidence_commands` repeated the same `recover-cycle`, `run-recovery-drill`, or `claw:health` step, so the replayable proof trail could be padded with duplicate evidence and still verify clean. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any runner-stability card whose `evidence_commands` contain duplicate commands while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if the verifier fails runner-stability cards with duplicate `evidence_commands` and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Runner-stability evidence commands must stay unique; duplicate recovery or observability steps make the replayable proof trail ambiguous without adding new evidence.
